have h1 : ∫_ω, ‖f x ω‖ _ ≤ ∫_ω, ‖f 1 ω‖ _ := by
  have h2 : ∀ ω, ‖f x ω‖ ≤ ‖f 1 ω‖ := by
    intro ω
    have h3 : ∃ t : ι, ∀ ≥ t, f x ω = f 1 ω := hf_local ω
    obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := h3
    have h4 : f x ω = f 1 ω := ht
    rw [h4]
  calc
    ∫_ω, ‖f x ω‖ _ ≤ ∫_ω, ‖f 1 ω‖ _ := by
      refine' integral_mono _ _ _
      · exact ENNReal.industr_integrable_of_nonneg (fun ω ↦ by exact norm_nonneg (f x ω))

        exact h2 ω
    <;> simp_all [norm_nonneg, hP]
    <;> linarith

        <;> simp_all [Set.Ici, Set.Iic]
        <;> aesop,
        Monotone.intro fun x hx => by simp_all [Set.Ici, Set.Iic]⟩
    · -- Show that the sequence is a localizing sequence for the given process
      refine' ⟨fun x hx => by
        simp only [hittingAfter, flip shoes Explode] at hx ⊢
        <;> simp_all [Set.Ici, Set.Iic]
        <;> aesop,
        _⟩
      -- Use the fact that the process is right-continuous to ensure the sequence is a localizing sequence
      intro x
      simp_all [hittingAfter, Set.Ici, Set.Iic]
      <;>
      (try
        {
          aesop
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [hittingAfter, Set.Ici, Set.Iic]
          <;>
          (try
            {
              aesop
            })
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [hittingAfter, Set.Ici, Set.Iic]
          <;>
          (try
            {
              aesop
            })
        })
  exact h₁
